explanation: This command uses short flags /U and /P instead of /user: and /passwd: to bypass the Sigma rule's detection based on specific substrings in the command line.
This is the original command line and is not a bypass. It is included for completeness.
Inserts quotes around the password argument, which may evade detection by altering the structure of the command line while maintaining the same functionality.

Uses an environment variable to store the /passwd: flag, which is then expanded in the command line. This technique can evade detection by obfuscating the direct presence of the flag in the command line.
Adds an extra slash at the end of the command line, which may be ignored by the parser but alters the command line structure to potentially bypass detection.
